name: Metro Holografix
image_prompt: Interior of a cluttered cramped cyberpunk pawnshop at night during a rainstorm, inspired by Neuromancer's Sprawl. INTERIOR FOCUS: cramped narrow shop interior dominates the scene. Tall steel shelving units along the LEFT and RIGHT walls floor to ceiling, packed with cyberpunk junk-tech: stacked vintage cyberdecks, dermal trodes, broken VR helmets, jewel cases, exposed circuit boards, coiled cables, neural interface jacks. A scarred glass display counter in the foreground with a brass cash register on it. A bare-bulb worklight on a frayed cord hangs from the ceiling, casting warm yellow on the counter. WINDOW IN MIDDLE-BACKGROUND: a moderately-sized grimy storefront window at the rear of the shop between the side shelves, with heavy rain pouring down it in clearly visible vertical streaks, water rivulets on the glass. Through the wet window, a glimpse of dark cyberpunk alley with bright pink and cyan neon signage reflecting on wet black pavement and puddles. Pink and cyan neon light from outside spills through the wet glass into the cluttered interior, mixing with the warm yellow worklight. Empty, no people, no figures. Absolutely no text, no letters, no logos, no readable writing or signage anywhere. Colors: warm jaundice yellow worklight on cluttered shelves, hot pink and electric cyan neon in the back window and street beyond, brushed steel, fingerprint-smudged glass, dust, wet black pavement glimpsed through the window. Cluttered crowded interior, gritty William-Gibson Sprawl atmosphere with prominent rain visible through window.
location: Metro Holografix, Finn's pawnshop on the underside of the Sprawl, several flights of fire-escape down off a Lower East Side alley nobody ever swept. The proprietor is a thin man who smells of Yeheyuan cigarettes and never throws anything away. The shop is technically retail but most of the trade happens in the back room. The back room is reserved for the working group.
time: Quarter past two in the morning. Finn does not keep hours. He has unlocked the door for them and gone upstairs to make coffee.
weather: The rain on the Sprawl outside is acid-thin and warm. The shop's air is dry and electric, the constant low static of too much old hardware running too close together. The radiator under the front window hisses and never stops.

atmosphere.lighting: A single bare incandescent bulb on a frayed cord over the front counter, throwing a circle of yellow that picks out the dust in the air. The rest of the shop in deep shadow except where neon from the alley outside leaks through the grimy front glass in pink and cyan stripes. The back room is lit by the green standby glow of a stacked rack of cyberdecks Finn has been meaning to ship for months.
atmosphere.sound: The radiator hissing. The constant near-subliminal hum of the cyberdecks. Rain on the alley window. A police aerial somewhere over the Sprawl, hunting. Finn's footsteps on the floor above when he moves. The faint click of cooling fans inside an old Hosaka. A wind chime in the alley that has not yet been smashed.
atmosphere.crowd: Empty of customers. Finn upstairs. The working group has the back. Out in the alley, two figures share a cigarette near the dumpster and have not moved in twenty minutes. They are not looking at the shop. They are not looking away.
atmosphere.smell: Yeheyuan cigarette smoke baked into every surface, hot solder, cold sesame noodles in a Chinese takeout container on the counter, the ozone smell of too much old hardware, dust, the chemical sweetness of new plastic from a recent arrival of grey-market neural jacks, instant coffee burning upstairs

layout.front_counter: A scarred glass display case running half the length of the shop, brass cash register at one end, hand-lettered cardboard sign reading 'NO BROWSE' taped to the inside of the glass. Inside the case: vintage microsofts in clear jewel cases, three generations of cyberdeck, a row of dermatrodes, a Russian cybernetic optics package still in its military foam tray.
layout.shelving_walls: Steel pegboard walls floor to ceiling on both sides, packed without system: deck cases, coiled fiber, broken VR headsets, an Ono-Sendai 7 with its case off, half a Hosaka, a stack of Sandii decks tied with electrical cord. Nothing is labeled. Finn knows where everything is.
layout.back_room: Through a beaded curtain at the rear of the shop, a smaller room with a green-felt cardroom table, four chairs, a desk lamp with a cracked shade, and a wall of stacked cyberdecks that Finn uses for trades the front-counter regulars are not supposed to know about. The room smells of old felt and new ozone. The wall behind the table has a mounted holo-poster of the Bahnhof, faded.
layout.fire_door: A reinforced steel door at the back of the back room with three locks and a peephole. Beyond it, a fire escape onto an air shaft that smells of fryer grease from the Vietnamese place two floors down. The peephole has been used recently. The door has been opened recently. Neither was Finn.
layout.alley_window: The shop's front window, painted half-black and half-grimed, faces the alley. The glass is cracked diagonally and has been since 2071. The crack splits the neon from the noodle joint across the way into double pink. Anyone in the shop can see who is in the alley. Anyone in the alley can see who is at the front counter.

objects_of_note: An Ono-Sendai Cyberspace 7 deck on the front counter with its top shell removed, custom Russian icebreakers visible on the daughter card. Finn priced it at twelve thousand and has refused to sell it to two buyers in the last week.
A jeweled hand on a velvet cushion in the front display case. Finn calls it the Hand. It does not function. He will not say where he got it.
A Yeheyuan cigarette burning in a chipped saucer on the back room table. Finn lit it forty minutes ago and has not been back.
A stack of currency-quality Berne Convention chip cards in a rubber band on the counter, denomination uncertain. Finn left them in plain sight on purpose.
A scuffed Sony Mao-Sat receiver hanging from the ceiling on a wire, pointed at a sliver of sky visible through the air shaft. The screen on its base is currently dark. It used to be on.
mood: Cluttered, watchful, and conspiratorial. Finn's pawnshop is the kind of place where every object in the room is a debt or a favor or a story, and where the value of being there is the access it implies. The shop knows things. The proprietor knows more. Frameworks brought in here have to contend with the fact that nothing in this room is what it appears to be on the price tag, and that the police aerial passing overhead every nineteen minutes is on a pattern Finn timed once and never wrote down. Decisions made here tend to leak. Finn pretends not to listen and listens to everything.
